insight: For South Korea, hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup have not provided the desired boost, as they failed to gain momentum in both of their breaks, with an average decline of 0.200 points per minute. Specifically, when they were pressing before a break, they saw a more significant drop in momentum, averaging a 0.300 point per minute decline, and were unable to regain their footing afterwards. In contrast, when they weren't pressing before a break, the decline was less severe, at 0.100 points per minute, but still, they were unable to capitalize on the pause to shift the game in their favor. Overall, these statistics suggest that hydration breaks have been a challenging period for South Korea to navigate, with the team struggling to maintain or regain momentum after the brief pauses in play.
